title: Our Family Road Trip Around the Island

sentences.en: Monday. Summer vacation officially started, and Dad announced we were doing something different this year: a full tour around the island by car.
sentences.zh: 星期一。暑假正式開始，爸爸宣布今年要做點不一樣的事：開車環島旅行。

sentences.en: We began on the highway heading east, and within an hour, the tall buildings behind us turned into distant, impressive mountains.
sentences.zh: 我們從往東的高速公路出發，不到一個小時，後方的高樓大廈就變成了遠方壯麗的山脈。

sentences.en: William counted every tunnel we drove through, losing count somewhere after twelve, which he insisted was still an impressive personal record.
sentences.zh: William 一路數著我們穿過的每個隧道，數到十二左右就數丟了，但他堅持這仍是個很了不起的個人紀錄。

sentences.en: Our first stop was a quiet fishing port on the east coast, where boats rocked gently and the whole town smelled like salt and fresh fish.
sentences.zh: 我們第一站停在東岸一個安靜的漁港，船隻輕輕搖晃，整個小鎮聞起來都是海水鹹味和新鮮魚貨的味道。

sentences.en: We watched the waves crash against the rocks for almost an hour, and even William stayed quiet, which almost never happens.
sentences.zh: 我們看著海浪拍打岩石看了將近一個小時，就連 William 也難得安靜下來，這種事幾乎從沒發生過。

sentences.en: From the port, a narrow road climbed up toward a mountain range, ending at a peak with a view so wide it felt like seeing half the island at once.
sentences.zh: 從港口出發，一條窄小的道路一路爬升，通往一座山脈，終點是一座山峰，視野遼闊到感覺一次能看見半座島。

sentences.en: "To the north, you can see the coastline," Dad said, pointing. "To the south, mountains all the way down. East is ocean, west is where we started this morning."
sentences.zh: 「北邊可以看到海岸線，」爸爸指著說：「南邊一路都是山，東邊是海洋，西邊就是我們今早出發的地方。」

sentences.en: That night, we stayed in a small countryside village, where the streets were narrow and every house seemed to know every other house's business.
sentences.zh: 那天晚上，我們住在一個小小的鄉村村落，街道又窄，每一戶人家似乎都對其他每一戶的事一清二楚。

sentences.en: Instead of a car, the locals mostly rode scooters and old motorcycles, weaving between narrow lanes like it was the most natural thing in the world.
sentences.zh: 當地人大多不開車，而是騎機車或老摩托車，在狹窄的巷弄間穿梭，好像那是世界上最自然不過的事。

sentences.en: We rented two scooters ourselves the next morning, and Mom drove one with William holding on tightly, looking both terrified and thrilled at once.
sentences.zh: 隔天早上我們自己也租了兩台機車，媽媽騎其中一台，William 緊緊抓著她，看起來既害怕又興奮。

sentences.en: By the third day, we reached a bigger southern city, where we left the car at the hotel and took the subway instead to save time.
sentences.zh: 到了第三天，我們抵達南部一座比較大的城市，把車留在飯店，改搭地鐵省時間。

sentences.en: The subway platform was crowded, but William was fascinated by watching train after train arrive exactly on schedule.
sentences.zh: 地鐵月台很擁擠，但 William 對一班接一班準時抵達的列車看得入迷。

sentences.en: We took a taxi from the subway exit to a night market, since Dad insisted no road trip is complete without at least one proper night market visit.
sentences.zh: 我們從地鐵出口搭計程車到夜市，因為爸爸堅持環島旅行沒吃到夜市就不算完整。

sentences.en: On our last full day, we drove west along a quieter coastal road, stopping to buy postcards at a tiny gift shop near the entrance of an old temple.
sentences.zh: 在我們最後完整的一天，我們沿著比較安靜的西岸道路開車，在一座古老廟宇的入口附近，一間小小的紀念品店買了明信片。

sentences.en: William wrote his own postcard to Perry, which technically makes no sense since Perry cannot read, but nobody had the heart to stop him.
sentences.zh: William 寫了一張明信片給 Perry，雖然這技術上根本說不通，因為 Perry 不會看字，但沒有人忍心阻止他。

sentences.en: By the time we finished our whole loop around the island, we had used a car, two scooters, a subway, and a taxi, in that exact order.
sentences.zh: 等我們完成整個環島一圈，我們已經用過汽車、兩台機車、地鐵，還有計程車，順序剛好就是這樣。

sentences.en: Sophia, who stayed home with Perry the entire trip, said she was glad someone finally saw the whole island instead of just talking about seeing it someday.
sentences.zh: 整趟旅程都留在家陪 Perry 的 Sophia 說，她很高興終於有人真正看過了整座島，而不是一直空談「總有一天要去看看」。

sentences.en: Lesson of the day: an island this small still manages to hide mountains, coastlines, quiet villages, and one very impressive number of tunnels.
sentences.zh: 今日心得：這麼小的一座島，居然還能藏著山脈、海岸線、安靜的村落，還有數量多到令人印象深刻的隧道。

quiz.question: What forms of transportation does the family use during the trip, in order?
options: A car, two scooters, a subway, and a taxi.
Only a car the entire time.

quiz.question: What does Dad point out from the mountain peak?
options: The coastline to the north, mountains to the south, ocean to the east.
Nothing; it is too foggy to see anything.

quiz.question: Who does William write a postcard to, even though it does not quite make sense?
